entries.title: More than 200 Iranian sailors stranded after US torpedo attack return home
entries.snippet: The survivors of the US attack on Iranian vessel Iris Dena, which claimed the lives of 104 people, were among those repatriated.

entries.title: 'Bit of pain' worth long-term security from Iran, Bessent tells BBC
entries.snippet: US Treasury Secretary said a "small bit of economic pain" was worth it to eliminate the threat of Iranian strikes on Western capitals.

entries.title: Trump's rift with Pope is playing out in public - it's costing him valuable support
entries.snippet: Leading conservative Catholics tell the BBC why they back the American pontiff in his spat with Trump.

entries.title: Magyar calls for fast handover of power in talks with Hungary's president
entries.snippet: Péter Magyar, the man who ended Viktor Orbán's 16 years of continuous rule, wants the new parliament to convene in early May.

entries.title: South Korea jails American YouTuber for public nuisance
entries.snippet: Johnny Somali sparked outrage after kissing a statue representing World War Two sex slaves.

entries.title: Four killed in second Turkish school shooting in two days
entries.snippet: There has been no official confirmation of who has been killed or what has happened to the attacker.

entries.title: South Africa names apartheid-era politician as new ambassador to the US
entries.snippet: Roelf Meyer played a key role as a negotiator during talks to end white-minority rule in South Africa.

entries.title: Australia's richest person must share part of her mining fortunes, court rules
entries.snippet: The long-running legal dispute is over a lucrative iron ore project in the top west corner of Australia.

entries.title: 250 missing after migrant boat sinks in Indian Ocean
entries.snippet: The trawler "reportedly sank due to heavy winds, rough seas and overcrowding", the United Nations said.

entries.title: Kanye West postpones France gig following UK ban
entries.snippet: It comes a week after the UK government announced it would block the rapper from entering the country.

entries.title: Australian pleads guilty to creating deepfake porn in landmark case
entries.snippet: The 19-year-old is the first person to be charged under a new national law.

entries.title: Starmer 'not going to yield' to pressure from Trump on Iran war
entries.snippet: It follows a warning from the US president that America's trade deal with the UK "can always be changed".

entries.title: Meghan to appear as guest judge on MasterChef Australia
entries.snippet: The Duke and Duchess of Sussex are on a four-day visit to Australia - their first since 2018.

entries.title: Israel and Lebanon hold first direct talks since 1993
entries.snippet: A US statement said the two sides had agreed to launch direct negotiations, at a time and place to be determined.

entries.title: Watch: Vance heckled as he chides Pope
entries.snippet: The US Vice-President was speaking at a Turning Point USA event, where he said Pope Leo should "be careful when he talks about matters of theology."

entries.title: Kenya fuel prices rise sharply despite reduction in tax due to Iran war
entries.snippet: Diesel prices rise by a record margin despite a reduction in value added tax.

entries.title: Man wins €1m Picasso painting in €100 charity raffle
entries.snippet: "How do I know this isn't a prank?" the winner asked when he answered a video call from Christie's auction house in Paris.

entries.title: Hamas rejects Gaza disarmament plan, Palestinian official says
entries.snippet: The official tells the BBC that the armed group will not move forward with talks until Israel fully complies with its commitments.

entries.title: Partner of US influencer who died in Zanzibar speaking to police as witness
entries.snippet: The police said Ashly Robinson, known online as Ashlee Jenae, had died after attempting to take her own life.

entries.title: JD Vance defends backing 'great guy' Orbán's campaign after landslide defeat
entries.snippet: Hungary's leader was a "great guy" who did a "very good job", the US vice-president said, but he was sure he could work with Péter Magyar.

entries.title: Trump says new talks with Iran will happen soon. And, Eric Swalwell faces new allegations
entries.snippet: President Trump says new talks with Iran could happen in the next two days. And, Democrat Eric Swalwell faces new allegations as a second woman comes forward accusing him of rape.

entries.title: Trump says peace talks could resume in days as U.S. military blocks Iranian ports
entries.snippet: President Trump said a second round of direct U.S.-Iran peace talks could resume in Pakistan within the next two days, even as he instituted a naval blockade of all Iranian ports.

entries.title: Fela Kuti is the first African artist to enter the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame
entries.snippet: In December, the late Nigerian superstar became the first African musician to get a Grammy lifetime achievement award. Now he's making history as well at Rock & Roll Hall of Fame.

entries.title: Tax season was supposed to bring big refunds. So far they're less than expected
entries.snippet: The average refund so far is $350 more than last year at this time, despite projections that it would be closer to $1,000 due to Republican-led tax changes as part of the Big Beautiful Bill Act.

entries.title: Pope heads to Cameroon as separatists announce 3-day pause in fighting
entries.snippet: Pope Leo XIV is heading to the central African nation of Cameroon with a message of peace for its separatist region and for talks with President Paul Biya.

entries.title: They counted on a rural dialysis unit to keep them alive. Then it closed
entries.snippet: A hospital in Nebraska shut down the only dialysis unit for miles, upending lives. That's despite a new federal program that gave the state more than $200 million to improve rural health care access.

entries.title: No one likes being discombobulated. How did the feeling get such a fun name?
entries.snippet: Sometimes you just need to recombobulate. That word isn't in the dictionary, but it is on a beloved sign at Milwaukee's airport.

entries.title: 'No peace': Nearly a year after her son's death, she learned that ICE was responsible
entries.snippet: Ruben Ray Martinez is considered the first person to be killed by ICE during President Trump's second term. His mother believes his death could have been avoided.

entries.title: Quit a GLP-1? Plan to start again? It's not recommended, but plenty of people do it
entries.snippet: A majority of people who start the obesity and diabetes medicines known as GLP-1s also quit them, and plan to restart again. Research hasn't yet shown the health impacts of cycling on and off the drugs.

entries.title: Morning news brief
entries.snippet: Inside the peace talks happening across the Middle East, Iran war drives Europe toward fuel crisis, Eric Swalwell facing new sexual assault allegations.

entries.title: Attorney says suspect in attack on OpenAI CEO's home was in midst of 'mental health crisis'
entries.snippet: The man accused of trying to kill OpenAI CEO Sam Altman by throwing a Molotov cocktail at his San Francisco home was experiencing a mental health crisis and has been overcharged by prosecutors, his public defender said Tuesday.

entries.title: WATCH LIVE: OMB Director Vought testifies on Trump's 2027 budget request in House hearing
entries.snippet: President Donald Trump has proposed boosting defense spending to $1.5 trillion in his 2027 budget released this month, the largest such request in decades, reflecting his emphasis on U.S. military investments over domestic programs.

entries.title: Israeli strikes on Lebanon continue as U.S. hosts historic diplomatic talks
entries.snippet: Mediators are racing to bring the U.S. and Iran back to the negotiating table amid a standoff over the Strait of Hormuz and a precarious ceasefire. Historic talks, however, did happen on Tuesday. The U.S. hosted the first face-to-face meeting between Israel and Lebanon in decades

entries.title: Talk of war dominates daily life in Tehran as ceasefire deadline nears
entries.snippet: To discuss how Iran's civilians are reacting to the latest developments in the Middle East conflict, Amna Nawaz spoke with special correspondent Reza Sayah in Tehran.

entries.title: Israeli-Lebanese talks more symbolic than substantive, former State Dept. official says
entries.snippet: To discuss the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, Amna Nawaz spoke with Barbara Leaf. She was assistant secretary of state for Near Eastern Affairs during the Biden administration. She's now a senior international policy advisor at the law firm, Arnold & Porter.

entries.title: News Wrap: DOJ seeks to erase Jan. 6 seditious conspiracy convictions
entries.snippet: In our news wrap Tuesday, the Justice Department asked a federal appeals court to throw out the Jan. 6 seditious conspiracy convictions of Proud Boys and Oath Keepers, Super Typhoon Sinlaku battered a group of U.S. islands in the Pacific, Gaza health officials say Israeli strikes

entries.title: Sexual misconduct claims trigger resignations and broader ethics concerns in Congress
entries.snippet: After separate allegations of sexual misconduct, two members of Congress are resigning. Democrat Eric Swalwell faces multiple allegations of sexual assault. He denies the accusations. Republican Congressman Tony Gonzales' resignation comes after he admitted to sleeping with a sta

entries.title: Study finds Trump administration cut legal immigration far more than illegal crossings
entries.snippet: President Trump touts a sharp drop in illegal entries to the country, but a Cato Institute analysis shows legal immigration has fallen even more dramatically, with 132,000 fewer people being admitted per month through legal pathways. Liz Landers discussed what's behind those numb

entries.title: New head of the Kennedy Center tries to show that the building really needs a renovation
entries.snippet: Trump's move to shutter the building for two years starting in July, which was approved by the board last month, has spurred lawsuits and an outcry that the closure is merely a response to plunging sales as artists canceled Kennedy Center performances in droves.

entries.title: Who is benefiting the most from the Trump administration's tax cuts
entries.snippet: Tax Day is on Wednesday, and President Trump and Republican lawmakers are trying to draw attention to new tax cuts and increased deductions approved last year. But with the war, higher gas prices and other economic costs, some polls suggest many Americans don't feel they're benef

entries.title: Illustrator R.W. Alley on how he brings Paddington Bear to life
entries.snippet: The origin of Paddington is one worth noting on Holocaust Remembrance Day. Michael Bond recalled seeing Jewish children arriving in Britain during World War II with name cards around their necks and suitcases in their hands. That inspired the beloved bear, and a Rhode Island arti

entries.title: Lena Dunham and Amna Nawaz explore public stress and trauma on 'Settle In'
entries.snippet: More than a decade ago, Lena Dunham rose to fame as the comedic force behind HBO's hit series, "Girls." In her new memoir, "Famesick," Dunham candidly, hilariously, and sometimes painfully, explores how she balanced her sudden celebrity with chronic illness, addiction and trauma.

entries.title: DOJ moves to erase seditious conspiracy convictions of Oath Keepers, Proud Boys in Jan. 6 cases
entries.snippet: In court filings, prosecutors asked the U.S. Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit to vacate the convictions so that the government can permanently dismiss the indictments.

entries.title: Mexico's President Sheinbaum pushes back on Trump over migrant deaths and Cuba
entries.snippet: The Mexican government on Tuesday protested the deaths of its citizens in U.S. immigration custody as President Claudia Sheinbaum pushes back against U.S. President Donald Trump's policies on multiple fronts.

entries.title: During visit to Algeria, Pope Leo walks in footsteps of his spiritual father, St. Augustine
entries.snippet: Leo's visit to Annaba, the modern-day Hippo, was a spiritual homecoming for the American pope on his second full day in Algeria.

entries.title: Carney suspends Canada's fuel tax in first act after securing majority government
entries.snippet: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney on Tuesday suspended the fuel tax in response to the Iran war in his first act after securing a majority government.

entries.title: Super Typhoon Sinlaku pounds remote U.S. islands in the Pacific Ocean with ferocious winds
entries.snippet: Super Typhoon Sinlaku pounded the Northern Mariana Islands for hours before daybreak Wednesday, slowing just to inflict more damage across the islands of Tinian and Saipan, home to nearly 50,000 people.

entries.title: Harvey Weinstein is going on trial for the 3rd time in a New York rape case
entries.snippet: Jury selection started Tuesday in the onetime movie mogul's latest retrial, where jurors will weigh whether he raped hairstylist and actor Jessica Mann in a Manhattan hotel in 2013.

entries.title: WATCH LIVE: CFTC chairman testifies before House panel amid scrutiny of prediction markets
entries.snippet: Commodity Futures Trading Commission Chairman Michael Selig is set to testify before the House Agriculture Committee on Wednesday.

entries.title: WATCH: Woman alleges violent sexual assault by Rep. Eric Swalwell in news conference with lawyers
entries.snippet: A California woman on Tuesday said she was raped by Rep. Eric Swalwell in 2018 and now plans to make a report to law enforcement.

entries.title: DW
entries.snippet: News, Analysis and Service from Germany and Europe - in 30 Languages

entries.title: Iran war: Iran military threatens Red Sea, Gulf trade
entries.snippet: The Iranian military said it will seek to disrupt trade in the Red Sea and the Persian Gulf if a US naval blockade continues. Meanwhile, Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon are trading fresh attacks. DW has the latest.

entries.title: Desert power: The promise and paradox of solar
entries.snippet: A massive solar tower in the Moroccan desert is the beacon of an ambitious push for a clean energy future. But fossil fuels and grid constraints stand in the way.

entries.title: Germany: Berlin conference for Sudan aims to raise over $1B
entries.snippet: The war in Sudan is entering its fourth year with international attention focused on other conflicts. The conference in Berlin is aiming to bring back some attention to help fund urgent projects.

entries.title: Hungary's Magyar hoping to form new government in early May
entries.snippet: Hungary's new government could take power in just a few weeks after Peter Magyar met with President Solyuk. Magyar also called on the Orban-aligned president to step down.

entries.title: Kanye West postpones France concert after UK visa denial
entries.snippet: Earlier in April, West was denied a UK visa, which led to the cancellation of his headline appearance at Wireless Festival. The entry denial was based on his repeated antisemitic remarks.

entries.title: Xi hails China-Russia ties, signals bigger role in Iran war
entries.snippet: Xi calls partnership with Russia "precious," as world leaders turn to China amid the Middle East crisis.

entries.title: Women's workwear: When proper fit becomes a safety issue
entries.snippet: With limited options, many women in trades and industry have to wear men's workwear. But a poor fit causes discomfort and can compromise safety. Now, some firms are stepping up to design safer, better-fitting gear.

entries.title: What we call sex work — and what it says about society
entries.snippet: A Bonn exhibition traces the cultural history of sex work and shows how the words used to describe it have shaped stigma, policy and lived experience.

entries.title: Germany: Man found guilty in Pelicot-style rape case
entries.snippet: A Chinese student has been found guilty of drugging and raping a woman in Munich over a period of months. The perpetrator was a member of a Telegram chat group where men organized rapes and shared images of their crimes.

entries.title: Hungary: What's next for Peter Magyar after election win?
entries.snippet: Peter Magyar has promised nothing less than a "system change" in Hungary. The expectations both at home and abroad are huge. DW explores what Magyar is likely to do on the most pressing issues facing Hungary.

entries.title: Germany news: Lufthansa air crew strike after pilot walkout
entries.snippet: Cabin crew from Germany's national carrier Lufthansa are staging another walkout, sandwiched between a separate strike by pilots. Meanwhile, a poll has found the far-right AfD well ahead in polls. DW has the latest.

entries.title: Pakistan secures $3 billion from Saudi Arabia as ties deepen
entries.snippet: Riyadh's funding comes at a critical moment for Islamabad as it prepares for a $3.5 billion loan repayment to the UAE. The funding is expected to help stabilize Pakistan’s foreign reserves.

entries.title: Nuba Mountains: a fragile refuge on Sudan's frontline
entries.snippet: Squeezed between Sudan and South Sudan, the self-governed Nuba Mountains are grappling with complex war dynamics while hosting hundreds of thousands of refugees they are scarcely able to support.

entries.title: India news: A Dalit officer highlights caste barriers in judiciary
entries.snippet: A Dalit officer says discrimination now operates through subtle workplace practices. Meanwhile, Trump calls up Modi over the Middle East conflict. DW has more.

entries.title: Turkey school shooting injures 16, teenage attacker dead
entries.snippet: The former student fired randomly inside the highschool. Among the injured are teachers and students. After being cornered by the police, the attacker took his own life, officials say.

entries.title: US: Maine pushes for first statewide pause on AI data centers
entries.snippet: While the move by the US state is temporary, it reflects mounting public fears over electricity costs, land use and environmental risks.

entries.title: Germany, Ukraine discuss drone deal as Merz hosts Zelenskyy
entries.snippet: EU membership for Ukraine, European involvement in peace talks and a comprehensive ​bilateral drone deal were all on the agenda as Chancellor Merz hosted President Zelenskyy.

entries.title: France's Renault slashes engineer jobs amid pressure from Chinese carmakers
entries.snippet: The French carmaker said it would cut up to 2,400 engineering jobs as the company tries to catch up to Chinese electric vehicles in terms of price and production speed.

entries.title: Spain: Sanchez government finalizes amnesty plan for undocumented migrants
entries.snippet: Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez's government hailed the plan as "an act of justice and a necessity." It will allow unauthorized migrants to apply for temporary residence permits instead, and to start paying taxes.
